reviews.review_title: I Wasn’t Ready for It to Be This Good
reviews.review_description:
I thought Book 1 exceeded my expectations… but Book 2 somehow surpassed it even more. They really elevated the experience and, in my opinion, made it even better than the first one.

The expanded abilities and variety of environments are honestly mind-blowing. Everything feels more imaginative, more layered, and more refined. The graphics are absolutely beautiful. Some of the best I’ve seen in VR. The world feels so alive and immersive! I also really appreciate that the puzzles are challenging without being frustrating. I’m not someone who usually plays RPG-style games, but this series works for me because it’s engaging without being overwhelming.

I’m so glad I avoided watching the full trailer because experiencing everything as a surprise made it even more magical. I won’t say too much to avoid spoilers, but if you truly enjoyed the first game, this is absolutely worth it. I really hope they release a Book 3 or something similar (not Glassbreakers), because this kind of immersive adventure is exactly what I’m looking for.

After having a great time playing the first Moss, I couldn’t wait to play Moss: Book II, and it exceeded my expectations. It’s fun, charming, and just as immersive and emotional as the original. I really hope they continue the story with Moss: Book III.
Best game I have ever played just the best. Has a great story enough challenges to puzzle your mind with every turn and just overall amazing gameplay I love the style of play best game ever!!
If your thinking off buying one do it

reviews.review_title: Changed my view point in gaming
reviews.review_description: Genuinely games just don’t feel the same anymore after I played this game. I thought fb t the first one was good but this one just wipes it out the water.

OMG... I just love Moss and Moss II. The mouse is just so darn cute. The graphics are steller and the story is gripping. The puzzles are sufficiently challenging so make you work for the win. Overall this is a five star game. One of the best I've played in Meta. I hope there will be a Moss III.
I have both moss 1 an 2, and played them twice i love them, and i will play again, graphics are brilliant the story line is great, cant wait for moss 3
Extremely boring and a way too long

reviews.review_title: Genuinely in awe of its brilliance.
reviews.review_description:
This game takes Moss and turns it up to 12. The story is phenomenal and had me crying one moment and ecstatic the next. Quill is genuinely my favourite character ever, she is the most adorable and lovable creature I have ever seen. Her struggle and story are so captivating, and I’m so glad I played this game. Everything is perfect, from level design to lore to character development. 

The narrator just makes this game work, her voices are both funny and heartfelt at the same time. 

If you buy a quest, you NEED to buy this game, it is worth the price and then some. You will fall in love with this world and its characters. I know because… I did.

Please Polyarc, for the sake of all of Moss… MAKE BOOK 3! I need more!
Just amazing.

erm.. I don't really have any words for this.  

I had previously played the demo and loved the look and feel of it, it does not do what it is like to play the actual game justice.  Anyone that has a meta quest needs to see what this game is like.

it's the kinda game that defines how story driven platform games should be made in VR.  Thank you for having created this.

I already own Moss 1 and loved it. I excitedly bought Moss 2 but got so fed up I didn't even finish it. That was a year ago so I figured I'd give it another shot. It's even worse than I remembered. I didn't finish it this time either.

On the plus side. this one has even better, more expansive environments than Moss 1 with the same degree of depth and artfulness. There are also new weapons and skills.

This could have been an awesome sequel if not for the many downsides: some of the weapons have special abilities but nothing tells you what they are or how to use them as tools to get through the levels. You have to just figure it out or stumble across their abilities - all while getting attacked (and repeatedly dying).

Many of the levels are MUCH harder to solve than the levels in MOSS 1. There should be an option to show better hints than Quill squeaking and pointing. I had to go online and find a walkthrough for some of the levels because I NEVER would have figured them out on my own. And once you find out what to do, a lot of them still take a good amount of skill to execute. It's so frustrating that even the ones I solved on my own weren't enjoyable.

Just like in Moss 1, there's a lot of backtracking through the same rooms so you're never sure if you're going in the right direction. 

If Moss 3 comes out, unless it's more user friendly, I won't be buying it. This one just isn't worth the effort.
I was so excited for this release.  Thank you just hit the spot.  I'll keep plying again and again for the visuals and challenges.
This is such a awesome game with really good graphics would recommend  this game to anyone im addicted it lol i hoping they bring more moss books out in the future

The moss series is a beautiful and wonderful must have for any quest user! It has a heartfelt story, gorgeous characters and a beautiful world. This is one of the few games in which you want to save the world because you care and not just because it is the objective! The story is full of emotion and is totally original! The whole story is well narrated/voice acted! The puzzles are just the right amount of difficulty for most people and the mechanics are not just incredible to begin with but evolve and change throughout the game. You would have to have a heart of stone not to love these sweet and adorable characters (especially quill)! I loved the game so much I finished it in 2 days! My only criticism I have is that they haven’t announced a moss book 3! It truly feels magical! If you don’t buy this game, you are robbing yourself of one of the best games you’ll ever play.
Wtf is going on with the stupid chakram it is possibly the worst mechanics of a game I’ve ever dealt with it’s impossible to work

I bought Quest 3 mainly for the sport games. I've never been interested in games like that. But this game is wonderful, visually beautiful, with interesting puzzles that aren't too difficult. I'm transported to a beautiful world, meticulously crafted down to the last detail. I never thought I'd love a game so much. And I'm 42! 
Naturally much better than part 1.

This is by far the best game on quest
Visually stunning - and one of those games you play till the battery headset dies and then cant wait for it to charge up again!
Would recommend to   everyone

This is undoubtedly one of the greatest games ever created for VR. An immersive, seemingly massive world. The cutest main character that you’ll fall in love with. Gentle game flow ripe with story telling. 

I was sad when I beat this one. I didn’t want it to end. I hope there will be a Moss: III.

I only got this game due to the meta+ subscription, I was definitely surprised as I've been searching the store for a decent rpg style game that wouldn't give me motion sickness.  Upon playing Moss 2, I was stunned, the amount of work that must of went into this game....wow.

  The game is stunning, just beautiful, and the cute little mouse is such a joy to play.  I wish there were more games like this available, I will definitely have to get the first Moss game now.  Strategic puzzles to solve, visually appealing levels, cute little storyline, simple enough controls, massive bosses to fight.  This game has it all and doesn't have the motion sickness most rpg style games on here have.

  Glad I stumbled onto the Moss games, great for kids but also enough so an adult can enjoy playing it as well.  Great job Devs, keep up the good work!  Maybe make a dungeon style game for us looking for a great hack and slash rpg, just an idea for you.

Besides the fact that I skipped through the actual book and the story telling parts, the game is quite addicting. I really enjoy that fact that you can control not only the mouse but also the bug enemies within the game. And I’m a stickler for looking for scrolls and sparkle/dust(?) as well so I enjoyed that there was an achievement element as well! Lastly the best part is that you sit down the entire game. This game is really good for people who motility issues or that can’t stand a long time (like me). 10/10 is a better rating and I would highly recommend it!

If five stars is the limit, this game qualifies for six or seven. I can not imagine the astounding amount of thought that went into this game. 

I’m an old gamer, but new to VR. I’ve been desperately hoping to find a game that makes me feel like I’m playing Zelda (specifically Wind Waker), but I wasn’t sure how it could be holistically done in a VR platform. This game did it. It did the HECK out of it!!! Speaking of Wind Waker, the way Quill opens a chest - you have my heart. 
Please, please, please make more!! 

EVERYthing about this game is mind-blowing. I giggle constantly. The story, controls, environments, gameplay - everything is top notch. 

Surprisingly, I have no complaints about this game! Trust me, I tried to think of some! 

PRAISE:
I feel like I’m controlling two beings: Quill & myself. It’s as if we’re working together. I speak out loud to Quill as if she’s my partner. 

If I wave to Quill, she’ll wave back (if I wave to Sahima, she crosses her arms in scowl! I love it!!). I’m not sure how to initiate it, but I’ve seen Quill point to things, telling me where to go. Sometimes when I’m stuck I ask (out loud) “ok, little mousy, what should I do?” - it would be cool if I put both my hands up as if to gesture “I don’t know”, & she would point. She wouldn’t give me the answer, but more work together as if saying “well, what’s that over there?” - Speaking of this, after a fierce battle, I wish I could hold my hand up next to Quill & she would high-five me. I’m surprised you didn’t include that ;) [UPDATE: I just beat the crab boss thing - Quill high-fived, low-fived, & mid-fived. I knew you wouldn’t let me down!] 

Superb level design. You end up back where you need to be in a very clever manner. 

I LOVE Sahima. She’s just like me (a total jerk). I adore that when she dies, she slowClaps a sarcastic “good job, idiot”. 


. 
Thank you, Designers & Devs - you’ve set an amazingly high bar. Get back to work!!

. 
HOLY COWS!! Did Quill just ASL to me that she’ll never forget me?!?! Just when I thought this game couldn’t impress me any further. omg, hire me to your team. I’ll work for free. 

I’m off to play the first one! Hooray!! MORE!!
This and the first game are excellent examples of what puzzle platformers can be in VR. Kudos to the developers, you are doing great work.
Looking forward to Moss III

Played this because of how good Moss Book 1 was. It didnt disappoint. New features to play with, new character to use.... such a great game. 
I can't wait for Moss Book III

I couldn’t give this game less than five stars if I wanted to!  The ingeniousness of the puzzles, the physical challenge of the combat missions, and the sheer beauty of the artwork are all unrivaled! ￼￼Polyarc really outdid ￼themselves. ￼￼I remember Moss l being good, but not this good. ￼I will say: Even though I completed all the puzzles and combat without any help, there were one or two times early on when I felt a bit lost and wasn’t sure where I was supposed to go. Also, I dare say the game was a bit on the long side. ￼￼Aside from those two things, this game is a masterpiece; ￼an inordinate amount of value for the price. Buy it —￼ you won’t regret it!
Simply amazing!

I think even death march RPG players are going to love this. I know because I’m one of them and Moss 2 fills a void I did not know was even there. It’s soothing but exciting, totally gorgeous, easy to master, great story(even though I skipped the beginning, I actually started getting really involved in the “books”).  It’s methodical & it sticks with you, if it wasn’t 2am I would still be playing. 

I played so long, I had to put heavy duty Aquaphor cream on my face l, it was so raw & red from  headset, worth every agonizing moment,m and the breakouts I will have tomorrow.

Edit: so I’ve finished the game, while wonderful and heart wrenching. Bittersweet like most endings are, the replay value isn’t here. I don’t know if having playing the first game would change this? Because  even though I didn’t collect all the scrolls for the stained glass windows you can see while “reading” the book, idk what they are for what they do or what happens if you collect all of them. That is my only complaint. But still five stars. Spoiler, it ends on a bit of a cliffhanger so I hope they will make a 3rd and that is when I will purchase the first game and play it thru.

reviews.review_title: One of the best games I've played.
reviews.review_description: I'm writing this after beating both Moss and Moss: Book II. Both were two of the best games I've ever played in my time on VR and they just did not disappoint me. It honestly felt like I was there the whole time, even in events where the plot seemed to happen without me. 10/10, would play it again on the other two files.

Very engaging game. About as close to being part of a story as you can get. Very well written and intuitive gameplay.  You really become invested in the lives of the characters. Moss and Moss Book 2 accomplish what playing a really good book would be like.
This is one of the best VR platform games out there. Great story. Great graphics and visuals. Great sound design. If you get nothing else, get this game.
Another amazing game from Lico!! Absolutely fantastic sequel to already fantastic game!! Keep em coming👍👍

Just a beautiful game. Magical world, lovable characters, amazing graphics and environment. Highly recommended! Great for adults and children alike. You really connect to that little mouse! Love it!! 🐭🐭♥️♥️
Every thing you expect from a VR game, good story, complex riddles and really feel into the game. Waiting for part 3 allready.
Loved the game got the game with horizon plus have not tried moss 1 yet. But moss 2 is amazing team did awesome job delivering awesome story with fun gameplay. Level designer nailed the game.
My wife got me to play Moss and I was immediately hooked.  MOSS 3 PLEASE!

reviews.review_title: Best game I've gotten to play
reviews.review_description: It's definitely the best VR game I've had the privilege of playing. It seems kinda hard to find the gems, but this one will forever live in my memory as the first gem to be found ✨️

This game has it all and in just the right amounts, the boss battles are epic.
the game really draws you in and gives you empathy for the characters. My heart went out to quill when his uncle died. 
This is how games should be, it drew me in made me care about the protaganist and left me wanting more.
PLEASE make a number 3

I just started playing VR games and I really love this one. The graphics are amazing! The battles are quite challenging, but I manage—and you can skip them if you want to. The interaction with Quill is wonderful as well.

this game graphics are just stunning, level design is perfect, charecters are adorable, lighting and colors are so lively and on point, and the music just completes the experience. I hope they keep making more books like this. Best game ever!
I loved this game! The visuals are stunning, game play is entertaining and the storyline really makes you emotionally invested in this cute little mouse.
Graphic is amazing but gameplay is poor designed. Story is like a story for 5 year old kid, rather not for me but small kids should be interested. There should be much more games in this perspective!
The life on that mouse is not good gave up and unistalled it also thought I would play the mouse not be watching from a distance

probably the best platforming game i've ever played in vr. good story, good voice acting, good gameplay. Docking a star because it hands out axhievements way too often. After every little level often. Other than thatc great game, highly recommend.
Waiting for BOOK 3
Love love love low key the ending is bad tho I wish I kept going
I love this game. Thoroughly polished, beautiful, and challenging.  i immediately started it over after beating the first time.
Very well made in all aspect
Love this game! Super cute character, challenging puzzles and interesting gameplay
lol actually play flight simulators and dayz..brilliant game
one of the most beautiful games I've played on VR
I love this game  ,nothing on this app come close to this game .
This game is not for me
Just Exceptional!!
Very beautiful game, easy to learn, fun to play.
So nice!
Beautiful.
We NEED Book lll!

I never imagined they could match the brilliance of Moss 1, but they have. Both games are the most immersive, magical, incredible gaming experience you’ll ever have. I’m so sad there isn’t a third, as there is nothing else this good in the world of VR….not even close. This game is simply genius…I can only pray they make more.
I cannot stress enough how important it is to play this game. Incredible use of VR, so gorgeous and fun, moving, it has it all. I want to erase my memory of it so I can play again. 10 stars

reviews.review_title: One of the best games in VR
reviews.review_description: The first game was already great, but this one is a masterpiece. Before playing it, I thought it was just a continuation of the story of the first game (which would already be great) but the game also adds a lot of stuff. There are different weapons, different puzzles that make use of the weapons, some very cool boss fights, and we can even play with a different character at some point in the story. Everything is extremely polished like in the first game, with pretty good soundtrack, great storytelling, and great graphics. I never thought I'd like so much of a platformer in VR, but the Moss series is absolutely mandatory for anyone with a VR headset.

I was wondering how this game had so many high reviews, but after finishing the game I can say it's deserved. The narration is amazing, the combat is simple but fun, the puzzles are challenging but manageable. My only complaint is that the controls for the menu and wall shimmying are very janky and unresponsive. Might just be me but it lasted the whole 3 days I've played. Other than that though, this game is perfect. The story was immensely captivating and I haven't been as excited to keep playing a game like this since I played Batman Arkham Shadow, and that's amazing. Definitely worth the play, and that's coming from someone that prefers first person VR games.
Love this so much! Did not want it to end.  Please come out with Moss Book 3.
Are you ever gonna make a sequel to Moss book 2 where we see what happens with the reader we are and the evil reader?

After dipping my toe into the vr world with classic starters like beat saber and first contact, this was my first real, full vr game. It completely sucked me in and blew me away. 

To be in the 3d world of your third person main character, while still playing an actual role as a human can not be visualised in 2d walkthroughs or reviews. 

If there will be a book 3, I will definitely buy it!

Motion Sickness: Same as the first game.  It is a traditional hack and slash game in a diorama style setting.  You can lean in and look around corners enhancing the experience over traditional gaming.

The game is a step up from Moss 1 in many ways.  There are a few equipment sets that alt appearance, new weapons and multiple boss fights of varying size.

It is around 3.5-5 hours range which I found to be a great value and not too long.  There are collectibles for replayability if you want.

There are some sections at the end that remind me of Portal.  Keep in mind this is brief  but worth mentioning.
What a beautiful game and gamestyle. Love it. The only annoying thing in the game is, that it only has one voice actor for all characters... Why?

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR games
reviews.review_description: Great visuals, captivating stories, and an immersive experience with good puzzles

This was my introduction to VR and I’m so glad—it was the perfect choice. The graphics, puzzles and storytelling were amazing. The setting and music were immersive. I found myself just pausing to look around. I purchased the bundle and played Book 1 and 2 back to back. Hoping for a Book 3 in future!

This is one of the first story/puzzles game I played on the VR and I’m hooked! Everything feels so magical in this game - the graphic, the story, and of course all the characters! Looking forward to more games like this from the studio!
Even better than the first game. More fleshed out mechanics - same beautiful world.
awesome game it's like playing a 2d scroller but in 3d and you interact with your character along the way. it's challenging but not too hard.
Super game.
This game is absolutely beautiful. This is how it's done.
Brutal

I can’t find a single fault with Moss 2. It builds on the near-perfection of the first game, removing the tiny frustrations that made it only near-perfect. I suppose a “hardcore gamer” might protest that it’s not challenging enough, but for me the difficulty level of both combat and puzzles in Moss 2 is just right. And when you do die in combat, or fall off a cliff edge, you generally only go back a few seconds to retry (the way ALL games should be). The story telling is even grander and more impressive in this sequel.  Was fully invested in Quill’s adventure from start to finish. Please bring us Moss Book 3!

reviews.review_title: Even better than the first one
reviews.review_description: This game is an improvement over the first game which was also a great game which I recommend playing first. This game is longer than the first and has more variety of scenery and enemies as well as multiple weapons that you fight with. I also found that this game has fewer frustrating parts than the original, but that could just be me getting better at the game.
Amazing beautiful game with a great story and more content! I wish for more games like this one.

I love the game Moss 1 and looking forward for Moss 2 💜
Amazing visuals, immerse yourself into the game and you are part of the game..
Now is i can get book 2 working..Its not going through 😲

Moss: Book II builds on the magic of the original, delivering a deeper, more expansive adventure that feels like another chapter in a Redwall-style epic. On the Quest 3, the improved resolution and performance enhance the beautifully crafted world, making Quill’s journey even more immersive.

The gameplay refines and expands upon the first game’s mechanics, introducing new weapons, more interactive puzzles, and larger environments. Combat is more engaging, with additional enemy types and strategic elements that make battles feel fresh. The puzzles remain clever without being frustrating, keeping a smooth flow throughout the adventure.

Narratively, Moss: Book II deepens the connection between the Reader and Quill, with rich storytelling and expressive animations that bring the world to life. The game’s emotional beats land well, making the journey feel personal and rewarding.

While slightly longer than the first game, Moss: Book II is still a relatively short experience, but every moment is polished and engaging. For fans of the original and lovers of immersive VR adventures, it’s a must-play that brings Quill’s tale to a satisfying new level.
